tool_call_sequence.description: Correctly labels the condition as primary parathyroid hyperplasia (a cause of primary hyperparathyroidism) to guide appropriate counseling, evaluation, and management.

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly using the phrase "primary parathyroid hyperplasia" and links it to "high calcium" in patient-friendly language.
Briefly explains the mechanism (excess PTH leading to elevated calcium) without contradicting established physiology.

Acknowledges that management options include "parathyroidectomy" and medical therapy such as "cinacalcet" (even if not prescribing at L1), and clarifies that definitive treatment is typically surgical when indicated.

Mentions key baseline evaluation elements appropriate to suspected primary hyperparathyroidism/hypercalcemia (e.g., confirm elevated calcium with albumin correction or ionized calcium; PTH; 25-OH vitamin D; creatinine/eGFR; consider 24-hour urine calcium to help exclude familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia; bone density and renal imaging when indicated).

Does not recommend inappropriate acute hypercalcemia interventions (e.g., IV bisphosphonate/calcitonin) unless symptoms/severity suggest hypercalcemic crisis; avoids unsafe advice such as thiazides or lithium to treat calcium elevation.

Provides safety-net guidance on hypercalcemia red flags requiring urgent care (e.g., severe weakness, confusion, dehydration, vomiting, arrhythmia symptoms) and encourages hydration unless contraindicated.

If discussing any medication options, demonstrates awareness of required safety checks before prescribing: asks about allergies and contraindications (critical) and considers drug interactions (non-critical), without fabricating results.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose primary parathyroid hyperplasia causing primary hyperparathyroidism and provide brief explanation and next-step evaluation/management overview.

steps: Summarize presenting features and labs consistent with primary hyperparathyroidism (high calcium with inappropriately normal/high PTH).
Explain the diagnosis using the terms "primary parathyroid hyperplasia" and "high calcium" and connect to excess PTH.

Outline confirmatory and baseline evaluation (repeat calcium with albumin/ionized, PTH, 25-OH vitamin D, renal function; consider 24-hr urine calcium to evaluate for FHH; assess bone/renal complications).
Discuss management overview including indications for parathyroidectomy and the role of cinacalcet for selected patients who are not surgical candidates or as bridge therapy (no prescribing at L1).
Provide safety-net/return precautions for worsening hypercalcemia symptoms and advise hydration and avoidance of contributing meds/supplements until reviewed.

acceptable_variants.description: Diagnosis recorded as primary hyperparathyroidism with explicit attribution to parathyroid hyperplasia in narrative explanation.
steps: Record diagnosis as primary hyperparathyroidism.

Recommend confirmatory labs and complication assessment; discuss parathyroidectomy and cinacalcet as options.

Does not recommend immunosuppressive therapy as routine treatment for CMS (unless explicitly justified by an alternative diagnosis), and avoids implying thymectomy as standard.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent diagnoses insomnia, prescribes temazepam short-term after safety checks, and refers specifically for CBT-I (behavioral sleep medicine) rather than general sleep medicine.
steps: Diagnose insomnia and explain the condition.
Complete allergy, interaction, and contraindication checks; prescribe a short course of temazepam with safety counseling.
Refer to behavioral sleep specialist for CBT-I.
